data.title: Sarepta rises on three-year Elevidys data
data.description:
• Sarepta Therapeutics (SRPT) is up ~6% in premarket trading Monday after releasing positive top-line three-year results for Elevidys (delandistrogene moxeparvovec) in Duchenne muscular dystrophy. 
  • Results from the phase 3 EMBARK study showed that Elevidys led to at least a 70% reduction in the rate of decline relative to the propensity-weighted external control group, based on Time to Rise (TTR) and 10-meter walk/run (10MWR).  
  • With a mean age of nine, patients receiving Elevidys achieved mean North Star Ambulatory Assessment (NSAA) scores above baseline three years after treatment.

data.title: Qualcomm-MediaTek competition heating up, Mizuho says
data.description:
• The competition between Qualcomm (QCOM) and MediaTek is heating up, investment firm Mizuho said.
  • “Our call noted growing competition between [Qualcomm and Mediatek] with QCOM entering more low/mid-end, while MTK remains aggressive on pricing in the high-end with D9400/9500, but QCOM still the leader,” Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh wrote in a note to clients. “We believe Samsung could potentially increase Exynos2600 deployments (QCOM noted at 75%+ penetration in Samsung) into 2026E with EU/KR. We believe the China RF supply chain continues to expand with Maxscend, Vanchip, and Hisilicon (via CETC, Runxin, SMEI) working to increase penetration into mid-end phones at CN OEMs.”
  • Rakesh reiterated his Neutral rating on Qualcomm, but lowered his price target to $160 from $175.

data.title: Packaging stocks are on watch on reports of containerboard price increase
data.description:
Packaging Corporation of America (PKG) hiked its containerboard price to $70 per ton, according to Jefferies. The price increase is set to go into effect on March 1.

Philip Ng highlighted that the firm expects the rest of the industry to match the increase in the coming weeks, especially if International Paper (IP) matches. "The timing of the announcement comes a little earlier than expected, with our contacts & RISI calling out an April window, but it's not shocking considering spring increases usually happen in early March or April," updated Ng.  

Notably, Packaging Corporation of America (PKG) has led the industry with a price hike announcement for January the past two years. Jefferies' view is that if the full $70 per ton increase is realized, major players such as International Paper (IP), Packaging Corporation of America (PKG), and Smurfit Westrock (SW) could see EBITDA jump by 10 to 20%.

As far as the overall containerboard market, checks by Jefferies indicate demand has been steady but seasonal, with demand picking up during agriculture season and the 9.4% of capacity closures starting to be felt.

data.title: NETSOL Technologies appoints Sardar Abubakr as finance chief
data.description:
• NETSOL Technologies (NTWK) has appointed Sardar Abubakr, FCCA, as Chief Financial Officer, effective January 20, 2026. 
  • Roger K. Almond, the company's current Chief Financial Officer, will remain with NETSOL as Chief Accounting Officer, responsible for NETSOL’s global accounting operations and the preparation of its Forms 10-Q and 10-K filings.
  • Most recently, he served as Vice President of New Business Ventures and M&A at Jazz, a subsidiary of VEON Group, where he led new venture creation, strategic transactions and major restructuring within a leading regional digital ecosystem. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Deutsche upgrades Cognizant to Buy ahead of Q4 earnings
data.description:
Deutsche Bank upgraded Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH) to Buy from Hold as they expect the IT services company to maintain durable growth above the industry average in the quarters ahead.

"The IT Services industry has been plagued by concurrent cyclical and secular headwinds over the past ~3 years, as the long-awaited pickup in demand has yet to materialize and GenAI has been an overhang on industry multiples," said Deutsche analysts, led by Nate Svensson, in a Monday investor note. "In this challenging environment where budgets remain tight, CTSH has positioned itself as a clear 'winner' from the shift towards vendor consolidation and cost transformation, which has manifested in solid and improving revenue growth."

Deutsche also increased its price target to $100 from $70.

"Under the leadership of CEO Ravi Kumar, the company has shifted its business model to focus on large deals of $100m+ in total contract value, which, although it took some time, has led to improving organic growth rates," Svensson added. "We attribute these share gains to the company's go-to-market strategy emphasizing actively proposing vendor consolidation, having a broad portfolio of services, and executing better than peers on leveraging GenAI (the company's '3 Vector' strategy)."

Cognizant plans to release its fourth quarter 2025 financial results pre-market on Wednesday, Feb. 4. A consensus estimate expects Cognizant to deliver revenue of $5.31B and earnings per share of $1.32.

data.title: AmpliTech group prices $9M unit offering
data.description:
• AmpliTech Group (AMPG) has offered 2.23M units at $4.055 per unit, in a registered direct offering for gross proceeds of ~$9.042M. Each unit consisted of one share of common stock, one Series A right to purchase one share of common stock at $5.00, and one Series B right to purchase one share of common stock at $6.00.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 27, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tiderock promotes William Waldrop to CEO
data.description:
• Tiderock Companies (TDRK) has promoted William Waldrop to the role of Chief Executive Officer, effective immediately.
  • As Chief Executive Officer, Mr. Waldrop will lead the continued expansion of Tiderock's services beyond its current industrial focus. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Satellogic announces pricing of $35M registered direct offering of common stock
data.description:
• Satellogic (SATL) on Monday said it has entered into a securities purchase agreement with a single institutional investment manager for the purchase and sale of 7.39M shares of Class A common stock at a purchase price of $4.73 per share in a registered direct offering.
  • The gross proceeds to the company from this offering are expected to be approximately $35 million.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 27, 2026.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for continued investment in Satellogic’s growth initiatives, constellation and satellite infrastructure, working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • SATL -2.85% premarket to $5.1.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Durable goods jump more than expected in November
data.description:
November new orders for manufactured durable goods: +5.3% M/M to $323.8B vs. +3.0% consensus and -2.1% prior (revised from -2.2%), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Monday.

New orders, excluding transportation: +0.5% vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.1% prior (revised from +0.2%).

New orders, excluding defense: +6.6% M/M vs. -1.3% prior (revised from -1.5%).

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Arbe Robotics announces $16M equity offering
data.description:
• Arbe Robotics (ARBE) has priced an underwritten offering of 11.5M ordinary shares at a purchase price of $1.40 per share for gross proceeds of ~$16.1M.
  • The underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.725M ordinary shares at the public offering price.
  • The Company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 27, 2025.
  • ARBE closed -16.7% at $0.15
  • Source: Press Release
